close_reason: Updated to use fixed timestamps with :now parameter
description:
## File
`apps/bedrock_job_queue/test/bedrock/job_queue_test.exs`

## Tests to Update (lines 66-78)

### 1. "expired? returns false for fresh lease" (lines 66-70)
```elixir
# Current
lease = Lease.new(item, "holder")
refute Lease.expired?(lease)

# Should be
now = 10_000
lease = Lease.new(item, "holder", now: now)
refute Lease.expired?(lease, now)
```

### 2. "expired? returns true for old lease" (lines 73-77)
```elixir
# Current (uses struct manipulation)
lease = %{Lease.new(item, "holder") | expires_at: System.system_time(:millisecond) - 1000}
assert Lease.expired?(lease)

# Should be
now = 10_000
lease = Lease.new(item, "holder", duration_ms: 5000, now: 4_000)  # expires at 9000
assert Lease.expired?(lease, now)  # 10000 > 9000
```

## Principle
Eliminate struct manipulation - use :now injection.
title: Update job_queue_test.exs Lease tests
